platform.name: Canva Developer Platform
platform.category: Frameworks, mobile, games, hardware, and creative ecosystems

assumptions: The developer has a supported computer, a modern browser, and an email address they can verify.
Git, Node.js v24, and npm v11 are not yet installed unless noted as prerequisites to install.
No Canva account, Canva CLI install, or Canva app project exists yet.
The aspirational route uses a public Hello World app rather than an Enterprise private team app.

developer_goal: Create a Canva account and reach the Apps SDK Hello World preview where the example button responds with Hello world!

first_success.outcome: The generated public Hello World Canva app runs via localhost, opens in a Canva preview editor, and responds to its included button.
first_success.observable_signal: Clicking the example app button produces the documented Hello world! response.

route_selection.rule: Use the self-service Apps SDK quickstart after account creation; prefer the first-listed CLI Hello World route over Connect APIs or Enterprise private apps, but do not count until signup and consequential CLI versus manual ties are fixed.

candidates.name: CLI hello_world with public distribution flags
candidates.reason: Displayed first on the Quickstart and supplies one command that sets template, audience, Git, and dependencies, but live account signup details and conditional Chrome local-network permission still block a verified count.

candidates.name: Manual Developer Portal Create an app plus starter kit
candidates.reason: Co-located manual tab reaches the same preview outcome with an explicit terms agreement, but adds more portal steps than the flagged CLI route.

candidates.name: Canva Connect APIs
candidates.reason: Separate OAuth and REST surface from the Apps SDK Hello World first success.

required_path.action: Create a free Canva account using the email signup route.

required_fields.label: Terms of Use and Privacy Policy acknowledgement
required_fields.notes: The public signup page states that continuing constitutes agreement; help-center email steps do not list a separate checkbox.

required_path.observable_result: The developer has an authenticated Canva account that can access the Developer Portal.

required_path.action: Install or verify Git, Node.js v24, and npm v11.
required_path.observable_result: git, node, and npm are available at the documented versions.

required_path.action: Install the Canva CLI globally with npm install -g @canva/cli@latest.
Run canva login, Allow CLI access in the browser, and paste the confirmation code into the CLI.
required_path.observable_result: The Canva CLI is authenticated to manage the developer's apps.

required_path.action: Run canva apps create "My New App" --template="hello_world" --distribution="public" --git --installDependencies.
required_path.observable_result: The scaffolded app is ready and the Developer Portal opens to the new app configuration page.

required_path.action: Change into the generated app directory and run npm start.
required_path.observable_result: The local development server reports it is running at http://localhost:8080.

required_path.action: In the Developer Portal Code upload page, set App source to Development URL http://localhost:8080 and choose Preview.
required_path.observable_result: A Canva preview editor opens for the app.

required_path.action: Complete any first-preview Open confirmation and conditional Chrome local-network Allow prompt, then click the example button.

required_fields.label: First-preview Open confirmation
required_fields.notes: Documented as required on first preview.

required_fields.label: Chrome local-network access
required_fields.notes: Conditional on Chrome and current permissions.

required_path.observable_result: The example app responds with Hello world!

prerequisites.description: A free Canva account with Developer Portal access.
Git, Node.js v24, npm v11, a terminal, and a modern browser; Canva actively supports current Chrome.

external_gates.description: Canva CLI browser authorization must grant app-management permission.
Public distribution later requires Canva review; private team apps require Enterprise and admin review, so public is assumed for first preview.

unavoidable_waits.description: Canva must deliver the email signup verification code before the account is usable.
CLI app scaffolding and dependency installation must finish before npm start.

platform_outcomes.description: Canva creates the consumer account after email verification.
The Canva CLI creates the Developer Portal app and scaffolds the local project.
The preview editor loads the localhost app and the Hello World button responds.

excluded.item: Reading TypeScript, React, and webpack fundamentals
excluded.reason: Documented knowledge prerequisite, not a product interaction in the measured path.
Quickstart Step 4 begins customization after the Hello World preview terminal.
Occurs after first interactive preview success.
Different developer surface from Apps SDK Quickstart.

sources.title: Creating and logging in to your Canva account
Canva signup
evidence_supported: Terms and Privacy acknowledgement on continue

uncertainties.question: Does the live Canva email signup transaction match the Help Center field list, or do additional CAPTCHA, identity-provider, or confirmation screens appear before Developer Portal access?
uncertainties.impact: Extra account screens change required fields, waits, and whether step 1 can be marked documented versus observed.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated read-only walkthrough or screenshots of a fresh canva.com email signup through first Developer Portal session.

uncertainties.question: Should the measured route be the flagged CLI Hello World path or the manual Portal create path, and are first-preview Open plus Chrome local-network Allow always required?
uncertainties.impact: These choices change action count, required permissions, and whether the Hello world! click is reachable without conditional gates.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Policy selection of one Apps SDK surface plus a fresh-account CLI preview walkthrough noting which first-preview prompts appear.
